what_it_does: Defines an authorship/provenance marking language: colour tokens for vi/human/suggested authors, a quiet left-edge marker applied via [data-author] ::before, an inline `.by` badge with author variants (.by-vi/.by-human/.by-suggested) each with a distinct glyph and styling, and a `.vi-authored` full-object tint for AI output awaiting review.
observations: [data-author]::before draws a 2px rounded left marker; vi is solid gold, human solid, suggested a dashed repeating-linear-gradient
.by badge: pill-shaped, 700 9px uppercase with letter-spacing, a ::before glyph 8x8
by-vi uses a gold gem (rotated 45deg square), by-human a round dot, by-suggested a dashed-border transparent pill with a round dot
.vi-authored applies --vi-surface as a subtle full-object wash for AI output awaiting human review
Usage documented: data-author on the object, or a <span class="by by-vi"> badge inline
standouts: Provenance framed in the header as a trust signal — making human-made vs Vi-made vs Vi-suggested legible everywhere in an agentic workspace
Three distinct visual languages (gold gem / round dot / dashed) so author is readable by shape, not only colour
Suggested author uses a dashed/repeating gradient to read as tentative/proposed
conventions: Token-then-rule pattern with role tokens referencing accent/zone primitives
Data-attribute-driven styling ([data-author]) plus an inline badge class family
Many fallbacks provided for referenced tokens (e.g. #3a5780, --accent-gold-soft fallback --vi-edge)
notes: The single home for authorship marking — edge markers + badges that distinguish human / Vi / Vi-suggested work, a trust signal for the agentic workspace.
questions: Where are the vi-specific tokens (--vi-strong, --vi-edge, --vi-surface) and --zone-source-* defined?
Is --accent-gold-soft defined, or always relying on the --vi-edge fallback?
What sets data-author / adds .vi-authored in the live app — a renderer or a manual annotation?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — header promises a consistent authorship marking language (badge + edge treatment) for human/vi/suggested; the file delivers exactly those markers, badges, and the awaiting-review wash.
apparent_themes: human vs AI vs AI-suggested legibility
intention_signals: Trying to make the origin of any piece of work visible everywhere in an agentic workspace — distinguishing human-made, Vi-made, and Vi-suggested as a constant, quiet trust cue, with AI output awaiting review visibly tinted.
novelty: Provenance as a dedicated design-token domain — encoding authorship in both colour and glyph shape, with a distinct 'suggested/tentative' dashed treatment and an awaiting-review wash.
contribution: Contributes the authorship marking vocabulary — author colour tokens, edge markers, the .by badge family, and the .vi-authored awaiting-review tint.
summary_for_embedding: ConceptV design-system CSS defining an authorship/provenance marking language: author colour tokens, a left-edge marker via [data-author], a .by badge family (vi/human/suggested) with distinct glyphs, and a .vi-authored awaiting-review tint.
intention_for_embedding: To make work-origin (human vs Vi vs Vi-suggested) legible everywhere in an agentic workspace as a consistent trust signal.
